title: Boat Rental in Vancouver: Your 2026 Guide to Choosing the Right Craft
html:
Vancouver's coastal waters, from Coal Harbour to the Gulf Islands, offer some of the finest boating on the Pacific. Whether you're after a nimble motorboat for a half-day outing or a crewed yacht for the weekend, prices typically start around 350 CAD for a few hours. Discover how to pick the perfect vessel and rent a boat through BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Vancouver

The city is ringed by well-serviced marinas that put you straight into protected waters. Coal Harbour Marina, near Canada Place, is the busiest departure point for English Bay. False Creek, home to Granville Island and the Burrard Street Bridge, offers sheltered access to the open strait. For those heading south toward the US border, the marina at Point Roberts provides a direct run to the Gulf Islands. Other popular bases include Vancouver Marina on the North Shore, Fisherman's Cove in West Vancouver, and the marina at Steveston in Richmond.

Which boat to rent

  1. Bowrider, Best for family days on English Bay. Up to six guests. Day rates from 350 CAD.
  2. Cabin cruiser, Ideal for overnight trips to Bowen Island or Howe Sound. Four guests overnight. From 550 CAD per day.
  3. Sailing yacht, Choose this for a relaxed afternoon in the Strait of Georgia. Up to eight people. Half-day from 400 CAD.
  4. Catamaran, Stable and spacious for larger groups wanting to reach the Southern Gulf Islands. Up to twelve guests. Full-day from 1,200 CAD.
  5. Motor yacht, For a premium experience with a skipper, consider an Azimut or Sunseeker. Ten guests. From 2,500 CAD per day.

If speed and agility matter, browse this speedboat on BoatHire24, a QuickSilver Activ 605 Open with 150 HP, perfect for exploring Indian Arm.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Vancouver?

Boat rentals in Vancouver range from 100 CAD for a shared tour to over 3,000 CAD for a crewed yacht.

  • Shared harbour tour: from 100 CAD per person, no licence required.
  • Small motorboat (half-day, under 20 feet): from 280 CAD.
  • Catamaran (full-day, six guests): from 1,000 CAD.
  • Crewed yacht (full-day, ten guests): from 2,500 CAD.

You can rent a boat through BoatHire24 and compare verified listings alongside owner reviews.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Vancouver?

In British Columbia, operators born after 1983 must hold a Pleasure Craft Operator Card (PCOC). However, many rental providers offer self-drive boats that include a brief on-the-water check. For larger vessels, a skipper is often included. Always confirm with the operator.

Tip: If you rent a skippered boat, you can skip the licence and focus on enjoying the scenery. The skipper will handle navigation through local tides and currents.

When is the best season for boating in Vancouver?

Peak season runs from May through September, when daytime highs hover around 22°C and winds are light. During these months, the predominately north-westerly thermal breeze makes for smooth sailing. Outside these months, expect cooler temperatures and stronger winds from the south-east, especially in the Strait of Georgia.

At a glance

  • Price band: 100, 3,500 CAD per day
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Guest range: 1, 12 persons
  • Currency: Canadian Dollar (CAD)
  • Common departure: Coal Harbour, False Creek, Granville Island

Questions about boat rental in Vancouver

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Vancouver?

Yes, many providers offer skippered charters or boats that include a PCOC check and a brief orientation.

How far can I travel from Vancouver in a day?

A typical day trip covers roughly 25 nautical miles, reaching Bowen Island, Howe Sound, or the southern Gulf Islands.

What is the cheapest boat to rent in Vancouver?

Shared tours on small vessels start at 100 CAD per person, while self-drive motorboats begin at 280 CAD for a half-day.

Are there family-friendly boat options in Vancouver?

Bowriders and cabin cruisers with seating for up to six are widely available and popular for family outings on English Bay.

Vancouver's waters offer something for every boater, from quick escapes to week-long adventures. Compare your options on BoatHire24, where you can also browse the listings to find the right craft for your group. If you own a boat, consider listing it on the platform to reach thousands of renters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Vancouver and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
